headline: Sabah Water Crisis Affects 800,000 Residents Daily
context: Persistent water supply disruptions in Kota Kinabalu and surrounding districts affected approximately 800,000 residents throughout 2025.

cards.big: 800,000 Sabah residents experienced chronic water disruptions in 2025
cards.sub: Some areas went without piped water for 72 hours during peak dry season

cards.big: Sabah Water Department recorded 2,300 pipe bursts on 1980s infrastructure
cards.sub: Non-revenue water loss stands at 58% compared to national average of 35%

cards.big: Federal government allocated RM1.8 billion for Sabah water in RMK-13
cards.sub: Only RM420 million disbursed by end of 2025 citing procurement delays

cards.big: KK water demand is 680 MLD but treatment capacity is only 520 MLD
cards.sub: Population growth of 3.2% annually outpaced infrastructure investment for a decade

cards.big: Chronic water failure tests whether basic services reach all Malaysians equally
800,000 people without daily water in an oil-producing state. The irony is the diagnosis.
